name: Embalse del Portillo – race from Castril de la Peña
name_original: Embalse del Portillo – carrera desde Castril de la Peña
region_intro: Jogging around Castril offers diverse running trails through a rugged landscape within the Sierra de Castril Natural Park. The region features varied elevations, from river valleys to mountainous areas, providing options for different workout intensities. The Castril River carves through the park, creating lush riverine forests and accessible pathways, while limestone formations and diverse vegetation add to the scenic environment. Best jogging routes around Castril The most popular jogging route is Embalse del Portillo – race from Castril de la Peña, a 2.4 miles (3.9 km) trail that takes about 28 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding natural park. Another top favourite among local runners is Enchanted Forest of Las Higueras Trail, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.0 km) path. This route leads through varied forest types, providing a shaded and natural running experience. Local runners also love the Nacimiento del Río Castril – Cueva de la Osa loop from Parque Natural Sierra de Castril, a 5.1 miles (8.1 km) trail leading through the natural park and past the river's source, often completed in about 1 hour 25 minutes. Jogging around Castril is defined by rugged topography, the Castril River and its pathways, and diverse vegetation and forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside runs to more challenging elevated trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 runners have used komoot to explore Castril's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Castril?
A: Jogging routes in Castril traverse a diverse and rugged landscape within the Sierra de Castril Natural Park. You'll encounter varied elevations, from gentle river valleys to more challenging mountainous areas. The terrain is characterized by dramatic limestone formations, ravines, and hills, offering a stimulating and visually engaging experience for runners.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, Castril offers several easy running options. For a scenic and relatively flat jog, consider routes along the Castril River, which often feature accessible paths and shaded riverbanks. An excellent easy route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986599497/embalse-del-portillo-race-from-castril-de-la-pena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Portillo – race from Castril de la Peña&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.4 miles (3.9 km) and offers views of the reservoir. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guazalamanco Waterfall loop from Mirador Guazalamanco&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.0 km trail.

Q: Can I find running routes with waterfalls or other natural water features?
A: Absolutely! The Castril River is a central feature of the park, with numerous watercourses, springs, and waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guazalamanco Waterfall loop from Mirador Guazalamanco&lt;/a&gt; specifically takes you past a beautiful waterfall. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2613975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de los Órganos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a notable natural monument in the area.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes in Castril?
A: Yes, there are circular options available for runners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31904281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento del Río Castril – Cueva de la Osa loop from Parque Natural Sierra de Castril&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 5.1 miles (8.1 km) circular trail that leads through the natural park and past the river's source, offering a challenging yet rewarding experience.

Q: What kind of forests and vegetation will I see while running in Castril?
A: The park's vegetation is diverse and changes with altitude. You can jog through areas with holm oaks and Aleppo pines in lower regions, transitioning to gall oak, ash, and maple in more humid spots. Higher up, you'll find bristlecone pine and creeping juniper. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989183384/enchanted-forest-of-las-higueras-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted Forest of Las Higueras Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading through varied forest types for a shaded and natural running experience.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more strenuous workout, Castril offers difficult routes with significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989175849/barranco-de-las-sabinas-to-guazalamanco-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de las Sabinas to Guazalamanco Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 6.9 miles (11.1 km) route with over 400 meters of elevation gain, perfect for experienced runners looking for a demanding run through rugged landscapes.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or points of interest I might encounter on a run?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover several points of interest. The remains of the Castril castle, overlooking the village, offer breathtaking panoramic views. You might also encounter geological wonders like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/183420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elías Gorge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5297752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castril Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features various huts and shelters, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3980308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo del Espino Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, which can be points of interest along longer routes.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Many routes in Castril, especially those with higher elevations, provide spectacular vistas of the Sierra de Castril Natural Park. A run up to the Peña de Castril, where the castle remains are located, would offer rewarding challenges and expansive views of the surrounding rugged landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Castril?
A: Castril's diverse landscape makes it suitable for jogging across various seasons, though spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. During these times, the weather is typically mild, and the natural environment is at its most beautiful, with lush vegetation or autumnal colors. Summers can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon runs preferable, while winters might present cooler temperatures and potentially some snow at higher altitudes.

Q: How many jogging routes are available in the Castril area?
A: There are at least 5 dedicated jogging routes detailed for the Castril area, ranging in difficulty from easy to difficult. These routes showcase the best of the region's diverse landscape, from riverside paths to elevated mountain trails.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging experience in Castril?
A: Runners who have explored Castril often praise the region's rugged beauty, the refreshing presence of the Castril River, and the variety of terrain. The constantly changing scenery, from lush riverine forests to dramatic limestone formations, provides an engaging visual experience. Many appreciate the options for different ability levels, from gentle, scenic runs to more strenuous, elevated challenges.
tips: The El Portillo reservoir or El Portillo Dam is located on the Castril River, in the municipality of Castril, in the province of Granada. This reservoir belongs to the Guadalquivir Hydrographic Confederation, in the Granada area.

Castril is a wonderful destination in the province of Granada!
Castril Castle: Located on La Peña, this castle offers stunning views of the town and its surroundings. Although it is in ruins, it can be visited on guided tours
Historic Center: Stroll through the cobbled streets and white houses adorned with flowers. Don't miss the Church of Nuestra Señora de los Ángeles
Sendero de la Cerrada del Río Castril: A spectacular walk through walkways and wooden bridges over the Castril River
Sierra de Castril Natural Park: Enjoy nature with trails, waterfalls and stunning landscapes
